title: Unit, Integration, CLI, Fixture, and Changed-Case Testing · Runtime Lens: Test Quality
summary: Tests assert only one public-domain English sample total, mock every filesystem call, compare unordered dictionaries, share mutable fixtures, and never run the installed command. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the test quality increment in risk-layered Bookbot test suite. Learners progress through predictio…
objectives: Prove tests fail under a deliberate changed behavior and protect against order, locale, encoding, platform, and stale-fixture coupling.
Test stdout, stderr, status, help, invalid arguments, working directory, and installed entry point as observable CLI behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-testing-fixtures-2
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-testing-fixtures-2.
# Case 7417: an assistive-technology terminal review.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts.
def worked_bookbot_testing_fixtures_2(unit_cases: int = 18, integration_cases: int = 7, cli_cases: int = 8, changed_defect_detected: bool = True, installed_smoke: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "corpusworked2",
        "policy2dekrow",
        "revision7417",
        "commandworked27417",
        "report2dekrow7417",
        "test7417worked2",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if min(unit_cases, integration_cases, cli_cases) < 1:
        return False, 0, "test-layers"
    if integration_cases < 2:
        return False, integration_cases, "filesystem-evidence"
    if cli_cases < 2:
        return False, cli_cases, "cli-evidence"
    if not changed_defect_detected or not installed_smoke:
        return False, unit_cases, "weak-suite"
    total = unit_cases + integration_cases + cli_cases
    assert total >= 7
    return True, total, "risk-layered-tests"

# Evidence: bookbot-test-quality-9o05r5

    evidence_axes_9o05r5 = (
        "corpus9o05r5",
        "policy5r50o9",

        "command9o05r53919",

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Prove tests fail under a deliberate changed behavior and protect against order, locale, encoding, platform, and stale-fixture coupling.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend the Test Quality model · Theory Test Quality, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Test Quality precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Defend the Test Quality model · Theory Test Quality, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory Bookbot team handles case 1971 during a clean-environment package release. Build deterministic Python evidence to layer unit, real-file integration, CLI, changed-defect, and installed smoke evidence; retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity; then replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Prove tests fail under a deliberate changed behavior and protect against order, locale, encoding, platform, and stale-fixture coupling.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Test Quality model · Theory Test Quality, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Code coverage percentage establishes that assertions detect defects.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: bookbot-test-quality-9o05r5.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: bookbot-test-quality-9o05r5(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_bookbot_test_quality_9o05r5\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?min\(unit_cases)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?integration_cases < 2)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?cli_cases < 2)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not changed_defect_detected)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
